title: Kingdom Made of Chairs
central_metaphor: A childhood fort made of kitchen chairs, representing the boundless, imaginative sovereignty of youth, which contrasts with the fragile, burdensome constructions of adult responsibility.
lyrics:
[Verse 1]
Sunday mornin’, static on AM
A skillet sizzlin’ on the stove
A cardboard box for a castle keep
At the end of a gravel road
[Pre-Chorus]
The world was measured in acres then
And a day was a century long
[Chorus]
We were kings of a kitchen floor
With a kingdom made of chairs
With a loyal hound by the screen door
And not a single care upstairs
[Verse 2]
Now the mortgage note’s a heavy crown
And the throne room’s fillin’ up with bills
That old hound’s just a framed photograph
On the sill above the windowsills
[Pre-Chorus]
The world is measured in minutes now
And the century’s already gone
[Chorus]
We were kings of a kitchen floor
With a kingdom made of chairs
With a loyal hound by the screen door
And not a single care upstairs
[Bridge]
I build real castles now, or try
From drywall, studs, and borrowed time
But I’d trade this key for a cardboard sword
And that kingdom that was mine
[Final Chorus]
We were kings of a kitchen floor
With a kingdom built on air
With a loyal hound by the screen door
And not a single care upstairs
